        have h₁₀ : 0 ≤ (volume.restrict (set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1)) {x : ℝ | ¬Irrational x} := by
          apply MeasureTheory.measure_nonneg
        linarith
      exact h₄
    -- If the measure of the complement is zero, then the original set is almost everywhere.
    have h₃ : ∀ᵐ (x : ℝ) ∂volume.restrict (set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1), Irrational x := by
      have h₄ : (volume.restrict (set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1)) {x : ℝ | ¬Irrational x} = 0 := h₂
      have h₅ : ∀ᵐ (x : ℝ) ∂volume.restrict (set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1), x ∈ {x : ℝ | Irrational x} := by
        filter_upwards [ae_restrict_compl_null (volume.restrict (set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1)) (by
          have h₆ : {x : ℝ | ¬Irrational x} ⊆ set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1 → (volume.restrict (set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1)) {x : ℝ | ¬Irrational x} = (volume.restrict (set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1)) {x : ℝ | ¬Irrational x} := by simp
          have h₇ : (volume.restrict (set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1)) {x : ℝ | ¬Irrational x} = 0 := h₂
          have h₈ : {x : ℝ | ¬Irrational x} ⊆ set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1 := by
            intro x hx
            have h₉ : x ∈ {x : ℝ | ¬Irrational x} := hx
            have h₁₀ : ¬Irrational x := h₉
            have h₁₁ : (0 : ℝ) ≤ x ∧ x ≤ 1 := by
              by_contra h₁₂
              have h₁₃ : x < 0 ∨ x > 1 := by
                by_cases h₁₄ : x < 0
                · exact Or.inl h₁₄
                · have h₁₅ : x > 1 := by
                    cases' lt_or_le x 1 with h₁₆ h₁₆
                    · exfalso
                      simp_all [h₁₂]
                      <;> norm_num at *
                      <;> linarith
                    · have h₁₇ : x > 1 := by
                        by_contra h₁₈
                        have h₁₉ : x ≤ 1 := by linarith
                        have h₂₀ : x ≥ 0 := by linarith
                        simp_all [h₁₂]
                        <;> norm_num at *
                        <;> linarith
                      exact h₁₇
                  exact Or.inr h₁₅
              cases' h₁₃ with h₁₄ h₁₄
              · -- Case x < 0
                have h₁₅ : ¬Irrational x := h₁₀
                have h₁₆ : Irrational x := by
                  have h₁₇ : Irrational x := by
                    -- Prove that x is irrational when x < 0
                    rw [Irrational]
                    intro ⟨q, hq⟩
                    have h₁₈ : (q : ℝ) = x := by simpa using hq
                    have h₁₉ : (q : ℝ) < 0 := by linarith
                    norm_cast at h₁₉
                    <;>
                    (try {
                      simp_all [Rat.cast_lt]
                      <;>
                      (try {
                        norm_num at *
                        <;>
                        linarith
                      })
                    })
                  exact h₁₇
                contradiction
              · -- Case x > 1
                have h₁₅ : ¬Irrational x := h₁₀
                have h₁₆ : Irrational x := by
                  have h₁₇ : Irrational x := by
                    -- Prove that x is irrational when x > 1
                    rw [Irrational]
                    intro ⟨q, hq⟩
                    have h₁₈ : (q : ℝ) = x := by simpa using hq
                    have h₁₉ : (q : ℝ) > 1 := by linarith
                    norm_cast at h₁₉
                    <;>
                    (try {
                      simp_all [Rat.cast_lt]
                      <;>
                      (try {
                        norm_num at *
                        <;>
                        linarith
                      })
                    })
                  exact h₁₇
                contradiction
            exact ⟨h₁₁.1, h₁₁.2⟩
          exact h₈
          ) h₂] with x hx
        <;> simp_all [Set.mem_setOf_eq]
        <;> aesop
      filter_upwards [h₅] with x hx
      <;> simp_all [Set.mem_setOf_eq]
      <;> aesop
    exact h₃
  -- Extend the result from [0,1] to the entire real line.
  have h₂ : ∀ᵐ (x : ℝ), Irrational x := by
    -- Use the fact that the rationals are countable and hence of measure zero.
    have h₃ : ∀ᵐ (x : ℝ), Irrational x := by
      -- Use the fact that the set of rationals is countable and hence of measure zero.
      have h₄ : (volume : Measure ℝ) {x : ℝ | ¬Irrational x} = 0 := by
        -- The set of non-irrational (i.e., rational) numbers is countable.
        have h₅ : {x : ℝ | ¬Irrational x} ⊆ Set.range (fun (x : ℚ) => (x : ℝ)) := by
          intro x hx
          have h₆ : ¬Irrational x := hx
          have h₇ : ∃ (q : ℚ), (q : ℝ) = x := by
            by_contra h₈
            have h₉ : Irrational x := by
              rw [Irrational]
              push_neg at h₈
              aesop
            exact h₆ h₉
          obtain ⟨q, hq⟩ := h₇
          exact ⟨q, by simp [hq]⟩
        -- The set of rationals has measure zero.
        have h₆ : (volume : Measure ℝ) {x : ℝ | ¬Irrational x} ≤ (volume : Measure ℝ) (Set.range (fun (x : ℚ) => (x : ℝ))) := by
          apply measure_mono h₅
        have h₇ : (volume : Measure ℝ) (Set.range (fun (x : ℚ) => (x : ℝ))) = 0 := by
          -- The image of a countable set under any measurable function has measure zero.
          have h₈ : (Set.range (fun (x : ℚ) => (x : ℝ))).Countable := by
            apply Set.countable_range
          have h₉ : (volume : Measure ℝ) (Set.range (fun (x : ℚ) => (x : ℝ))) = 0 := by
            apply MeasureTheory.measure_zero_of_countable
            <;> simp_all [h₈]
          exact h₉
        have h₈ : (volume : Measure ℝ) {x : ℝ | ¬Irrational x} ≤ 0 := by
          linarith
        have h₉ : 0 ≤ (volume : Measure ℝ) {x : ℝ | ¬Irrational x} := by
          apply MeasureTheory.measure_nonneg
        linarith
      -- If the measure of the complement is zero, then the original set is almost everywhere.
      have h₅ : ∀ᵐ (x : ℝ), x ∈ {x : ℝ | Irrational x} := by
        have h₆ : (volume : Measure ℝ) {x : ℝ | ¬Irrational x} = 0 := h₄
        have h₇ : ∀ᵐ (x : ℝ), x ∈ {x : ℝ | Irrational x} := by
          filter_upwards [ae_restrict_compl_null volume (by simp [h₆])] with x hx
          <;> simp_all [Set.mem_setOf_eq]
          <;> aesop
        exact h₇
      filter_upwards [h₅] with x hx
      <;> simp_all [Set.mem_setOf_eq]
      <;> aesop
    exact h₃
  exact h₂

have h : ∀ᵐ (x : ℝ) ∂volume.restrict (Set.univ : Set ℝ), Irrational x := by
    have h₁ : ∀ᵐ (x : ℝ) ∂volume.restrict (Set.univ : Set ℝ), Irrational x := by
      -- Use the fact that the irrational numbers are dense in ℝ to show that almost every x is irrational.
      have h₂ : Irrational ∼[volume.restrict (Set.univ : Set ℝ)] Set.univ := by
        -- The irrational numbers are dense in ℝ, so they are equal almost everywhere.
        have h₃ : volume.restrict (Set.univ : Set ℝ) = volume := by
          simp
        rw [h₃]
        -- Use the fact that the set of irrational numbers has full measure in ℝ.
        have h₄ : Irrational ∼[volume] Set.univ := by
          -- The irrational numbers have full measure in ℝ.
          have h₅ : volume (Set.univ : Set ℝ) = ∞ := by
            simp [Real.volume_univ]
          have h₆ : volume (Irrational : Set ℝ) = ∞ := by
            -- The set of irrational numbers is uncountable and has full measure.
            have h₇ : Irrational = Set.univ \ (Set.range (coe : ℚ → ℝ)) := by
              ext x
              simp [Irrational, Set.mem_range]
              <;>
              tauto
            rw [h₇]
            -- Use the fact that the complement of a countable set in ℝ has full measure.
            have h₈ : volume (Set.range (coe : ℚ → ℝ)) = 0 := by
              -- The set of rationals is countable, so it has measure zero.
              have h₉ : volume (Set.range (coe : ℚ → ℝ)) = 0 := by
                -- Use the fact that the set of rationals is countable.
                have h₁₀ : Set.Countable (Set.range (coe : ℚ → ℝ)) := by
                  apply Set.countable_range
                  <;>
                  infer_instance
                -- Use the fact that a countable set has measure zero.
                exact MeasureTheory.volume_zero_of_countable (by exact h₁₀)
              exact h₉
            -- Use the fact that the complement of a measure-zero set has full measure.
            have h₉ : volume (Set.univ \ (Set.range (coe : ℚ → ℝ)) : Set ℝ) = ∞ := by
              -- Use the fact that the complement of a measure-zero set has full measure.
              have h₁₀ : volume (Set.univ \ (Set.range (coe : ℚ → ℝ)) : Set ℝ) = volume (Set.univ : Set ℝ) := by
                -- Use the fact that the complement of a measure-zero set has full measure.
                rw [MeasureTheory.volume_sdiff (by
                  -- Show that the set of rationals is a subset of the real numbers.
                  have h₁₁ : (Set.range (coe : ℚ → ℝ)) ⊆ (Set.univ : Set ℝ) := by
                    -- Show that the set of rationals is a subset of the real numbers.
                    exact Set.subset_univ _
                  exact h₁₁) (by
                  -- Show that the set of rationals has measure zero.
                  have h₁₁ : volume (Set.range (coe : ℚ → ℝ)) = 0 := by
                    -- Use the fact that the set of rationals is countable.
                    have h₁₂ : Set.Countable (Set.range (coe : ℚ → ℝ)) := by
                      apply Set.countable_range
                      <;>
                      infer_instance
                    -- Use the fact that a countable set has measure zero.
                    exact MeasureTheory.volume_zero_of_countable (by exact h₁₂)
                  simp [h₁₁])]
                <;>
                simp
              rw [h₁₀]
              -- Use the fact that the volume of the real numbers is infinite.
              simp [Real.volume_univ]
            exact h₉
          -- Use the fact that the set of irrational numbers has full measure in ℝ.
          have h₁₀ : Irrational ∼[volume] Set.univ := by
            -- Use the fact that the set of irrational numbers has full measure in ℝ.
            have h₁₁ : volume (Irrational : Set ℝ) = volume (Set.univ : Set ℝ) := by
              -- Use the fact that the set of irrational numbers has full measure in ℝ.
              rw [h₆]
              simp [h₅]
            -- Use the fact that the set of irrational numbers has full measure in ℝ.
            exact h₁₁.symm ▸ MeasureTheory.ae_univ
          exact h₁₀
        exact h₄
      -- Use the fact that the set of irrational numbers has full measure in ℝ.
      have h₃ : ∀ᵐ (x : ℝ) ∂volume.restrict (Set.univ : Set ℝ), Irrational x := by
        -- Use the fact that the set of irrational numbers has full measure in ℝ.
        have h₄ : Irrational ∼[volume.restrict (Set.univ : Set ℝ)] Set.univ := h₂
        -- Use the fact that the set of irrational numbers has full measure in ℝ.
        have h₅ : ∀ᵐ (x : ℝ) ∂volume.restrict (Set.univ : Set ℝ), x ∈ Irrational := by
          -- Use the fact that the set of irrational numbers has full measure in ℝ.
          have h₆ : Irrational ∼[volume.restrict (Set.univ : Set ℝ)] Set.univ := h₂
          -- Use the fact that the set of irrational numbers has full measure in ℝ.
          exact h₆.ae
        -- Use the fact that the set of irrational numbers has full measure in ℝ.
        filter_upwards [h₅] with x hx
        -- Use the fact that the set of irrational numbers has full measure in ℝ.
        exact hx
      exact h₃
    exact h₁
  -- Use the fact that the set of irrational numbers has full measure in ℝ.
  have h₁ : ∀ᵐ (x : ℝ), Irrational x := by
    -- Use the fact that the set of irrational numbers has full measure in ℝ.
    have h₂ : ∀ᵐ (x : ℝ) ∂volume.restrict (Set.univ : Set ℝ), Irrational x := h
    -- Use the fact that the set of irrational numbers has full measure in ℝ.
    have h₃ : ∀ᵐ (x : ℝ), Irrational x := by
      -- Use the fact that the set of irrational numbers has full measure in ℝ.
      have h₄ : volume.restrict (Set.univ : Set ℝ) = volume := by
        simp
      -- Use the fact that the set of irrational numbers has full measure in ℝ.
      have h₅ : ∀ᵐ (x : ℝ) ∂volume, Irrational x := by
        -- Use the fact that the set of irrational numbers has full measure in ℝ.
        simpa [h₄] using h₂
      -- Use the fact that the set of irrational numbers has full measure in ℝ.
      exact h₅
    -- Use the fact that the set of irrational numbers has full measure in ℝ.
    exact h₃
  -- Use the fact that the set of irrational numbers has full measure in ℝ.
  exact h₁

have h : ∀ᵐ (x : ℝ), Irrational x := by
    have h₁ : ∀ᵐ (x : ℝ), Irrational x := by
      -- Use the fact that the set of rational numbers is countable and has measure zero
      have h₂ : {x : ℝ | ¬Irrational x} = Set.range (fun (q : ℚ) => (q : ℝ)) := by
        ext x
        simp [Irrational, Set.mem_range]
        <;>
        (try { norm_num }) <;>
        (try {
          constructor <;>
          intro h <;>
          (try { cases' h with q hq <;> refine' ⟨q, _⟩ <;> simp_all [Rat.cast_inj] }) <;>
          (try {
            obtain ⟨q, hq⟩ := h
            refine' ⟨q, _⟩ <;> simp_all [Rat.cast_inj]
          })
        }) <;>
        (try {
          by_cases h : ∃ (q : ℚ), (q : ℝ) = x <;>
          simp_all [Irrational, Set.mem_range]
          <;>
          (try { aesop })
        })
      -- The set of rational numbers is countable and has measure zero
      have h₃ : {x : ℝ | ¬Irrational x}.countable := by
        rw [h₂]
        exact Set.countable_range _
      -- The set of rational numbers has measure zero
      have h₄ : MeasureTheory.Measure (volume : Measure ℝ) {x : ℝ | ¬Irrational x} = 0 := by
        have h₅ : {x : ℝ | ¬Irrational x}.countable := h₃
        have h₆ : MeasureTheory.Measure (volume : Measure ℝ) {x : ℝ | ¬Irrational x} = 0 := by
          apply MeasureTheory.measure_countable
          <;> simp_all [h₂]
          <;>
          (try { aesop })
        exact h₆
      -- The complement of the set of rational numbers is the set of irrational numbers
      have h₅ : ∀ᵐ (x : ℝ), Irrational x := by
        have h₆ : MeasureTheory.Measure (volume : Measure ℝ) {x : ℝ | ¬Irrational x} = 0 := h₄
        have h₇ : ∀ᵐ (x : ℝ), ¬(¬Irrational x) := by
          apply MeasureTheory.ae_of_measure_zero
          <;> simp_all [h₂]
          <;>
          (try { aesop })
        filter_upwards [h₇] with x hx
        <;> simp_all [not_not]
        <;>
        (try { aesop })
      exact h₅
    exact h₁
  exact h

have h : hasFullMeasure (volume : Measure ℝ) {x : ℝ | Irrational x} := by
    have h₁ : hasFullMeasure (volume : Measure ℝ) {x : ℝ | Irrational x} := by
      -- Use the fact that the set of irrational numbers is full measure
      -- because the set of rational numbers is countable and has measure zero.
      have h₂ : {x : ℝ | Irrational x} = {x : ℝ | ¬(∃ (q : ℚ), (q : ℝ) = x)} := by
        ext x
        simp [Irrational, not_exists]
        <;>
        (try { aesop }) <;>
        (try { simp_all }) <;>
        (try { norm_cast }) <;>
        (try { aesop })
      rw [h₂]
      -- The set of rationals is countable and has measure zero.
      have h₃ : {x : ℝ | ¬(∃ (q : ℚ), (q : ℝ) = x)} = {x : ℝ | ¬(∃ (q : ℚ), (q : ℝ) = x)} := rfl
      rw [h₃]
      -- Use the fact that the set of rationals is countable and has measure zero.
      have h₄ : {x : ℝ | (∃ (q : ℚ), (q : ℝ) = x)} ⊆ {x : ℝ | (∃ (q : ℚ), (q : ℝ) = x)} := by
        intro x hx
        exact hx
      have h₅ : {x : ℝ | (∃ (q : ℚ), (q : ℝ) = x)} = ⋃ (q : ℚ), ({(q : ℝ)} : Set ℝ) := by
        ext x
        simp [exists_prop]
        <;>
        (try { aesop }) <;>
        (try { simp_all }) <;>
        (try { norm_cast }) <;>
        (try { aesop })
      rw [h₅] at h₄
      -- The set of rationals is countable and has measure zero.
      have h₆ : (volume : Measure ℝ) (⋃ (q : ℚ), ({(q : ℝ)} : Set ℝ)) = 0 := by
        -- The set of rationals is countable and each element has measure zero.
        have h₇ : (volume : Measure ℝ) (⋃ (q : ℚ), ({(q : ℝ)} : Set ℝ)) ≤ ∑' (q : ℚ), (volume : Measure ℝ) ({(q : ℝ)} : Set ℝ) := by
          -- The measure of the union is less than or equal to the sum of the measures.
          exact (volume : Measure ℝ).MeasureTheory.measure_biUnion_le _
        have h₈ : ∑' (q : ℚ), (volume : Measure ℝ) ({(q : ℝ)} : Set ℝ) = 0 := by
          -- The sum of the measures of the singletons is zero.
          have h₉ : ∀ (q : ℚ), (volume : Measure ℝ) ({(q : ℝ)} : Set ℝ) = 0 := by
            intro q
            -- Each singleton has measure zero.
            have h₁₀ : (volume : Measure ℝ) ({(q : ℝ)} : Set ℝ) = 0 := by
              simpa using volume_singleton (q : ℝ)
            exact h₁₀
          -- The sum of the measures of the singletons is zero.
          calc
            ∑' (q : ℚ), (volume : Measure ℝ) ({(q : ℝ)} : Set ℝ) = ∑' (q : ℚ), 0 := by
              congr with q
              rw [h₉ q]
            _ = 0 := by simp
        have h₁₀ : (volume : Measure ℝ) (⋃ (q : ℚ), ({(q : ℝ)} : Set ℝ)) ≤ 0 := by
          linarith
        have h₁₁ : (volume : Measure ℝ) (⋃ (q : ℚ), ({(q : ℝ)} : Set ℝ)) ≥ 0 := by
          apply Measure.zero_le
        -- The measure of the union is zero.
        linarith
      -- The set of irrationals is full measure.
      have h₇ : hasFullMeasure (volume : Measure ℝ) {x : ℝ | ¬(∃ (q : ℚ), (q : ℝ) = x)} := by
        have h₈ : {x : ℝ | (∃ (q : ℚ), (q : ℝ) = x)} ⊆ (⋃ (q : ℚ), ({(q : ℝ)} : Set ℝ)) := by
          intro x hx
          -- Each x is in one of the singletons.
          obtain ⟨q, rfl⟩ := hx
          exact Set.mem_iUnion.mpr ⟨q, by simp⟩
        have h₉ : (volume : Measure ℝ) (⋃ (q : ℚ), ({(q : ℝ)} : Set ℝ)) = 0 := h₆
        have h₁₀ : (volume : Measure ℝ) {x : ℝ | (∃ (q : ℚ), (q : ℝ) = x)} ≤ 0 := by
          calc
            (volume : Measure ℝ) {x : ℝ | (∃ (q : ℚ), (q : ℝ) = x)} ≤ (volume : Measure ℝ) (⋃ (q : ℚ), ({(q : ℝ)} : Set ℝ)) := by
              apply Measure.measure_mono
              exact h₈
            _ = 0 := by rw [h₉]
            _ ≤ 0 := by linarith
        have h₁₁ : (volume : Measure ℝ) {x : ℝ | ¬(∃ (q : ℚ), (q : ℝ) = x)} = (volume : Measure ℝ) (Set.univ : Set ℝ) := by
          have h₁₂ : (volume : Measure ℝ) {x : ℝ | ¬(∃ (q : ℚ), (q : ℝ) = x)} = (volume : Measure ℝ) (Set.univ : Set ℝ) := by
            have h₁₃ : {x : ℝ | ¬(∃ (q : ℚ), (q : ℝ) = x)} = (Set.univ : Set ℝ) \ {x : ℝ | (∃ (q : ℚ), (q : ℝ) = x)} := by
              ext x
              simp [Set.mem_diff, Set.mem_univ, true_and]
              <;>
              (try { aesop }) <;>
              (try { simp_all }) <;>
              (try { norm_cast }) <;>
              (try { aesop })
            rw [h₁₃]
            have h₁₄ : (volume : Measure ℝ) ((Set.univ : Set ℝ) \ {x : ℝ | (∃ (q : ℚ), (q : ℝ) = x)}) = (volume : Measure ℝ) (Set.univ : Set ℝ) := by
              have h₁₅ : (volume : Measure ℝ) {x : ℝ | (∃ (q : ℚ), (q : ℝ) = x)} = 0 := by
                linarith
              have h₁₆ : (volume : Measure ℝ) ((Set.univ : Set ℝ) \ {x : ℝ | (∃ (q : ℚ), (q : ℝ) = x)}) = (volume : Measure ℝ) (Set.univ : Set ℝ) := by
                have h₁₇ : (volume : Measure ℝ) {x : ℝ | (∃ (q : ℚ), (q : ℝ) = x)} = 0 := by
                  linarith
                have h₁₈ : (volume : Measure ℝ) ((Set.univ : Set ℝ) \ {x : ℝ | (∃ (q : ℚ), (q : ℝ) = x)}) = (volume : Measure ℝ) (Set.univ : Set ℝ) := by
                  -- Use the fact that the measure of the set of rationals is zero.
                  rw [measure_sdiff] <;>
                  (try { simp_all [MeasureTheory.Measure.antitone] }) <;>
                  (try { linarith }) <;>
                  (try { exact trivial }) <;>
                  (try {
                    have h₁₉ : {x : ℝ | (∃ (q : ℚ), (q : ℝ) = x)} ⊆ (Set.univ : Set ℝ) := by
                      intro x hx
                      simp at hx ⊢
                      <;>
                      (try { aesop }) <;>
                      (try { simp_all }) <;>
                      (try { norm_cast }) <;>
                      (try { aesop })
                    exact h₁₉
                  })
                  <;>
                  (try { linarith })
                exact h₁₈
              exact h₁₆
            rw [h₁₄]
          rw [h₁₂]
        have h₁₂ : hasFullMeasure (volume : Measure ℝ) {x : ℝ | ¬(∃ (q : ℚ), (q : ℝ) = x)} := by
          rw [hasFullMeasure_iff]
          have h₁₃ : (volume : Measure ℝ) {x : ℝ | ¬(∃ (q : ℚ), (q : ℝ) = x)} = (volume : Measure ℝ) (Set.univ : Set ℝ) := h₁₁
          have h₁₄ : (volume : Measure ℝ) (Set.univ : Set ℝ) = (volume : Measure ℝ) (Set.univ : Set ℝ) := rfl
          have h₁₅ : (volume : Measure ℝ) (Set.univ : Set ℝ) ≠ 0 := by
            have h₁₆ : (volume : Measure ℝ) (Set.univ : Set ℝ) = (⊤ : ℝ≥0∞) := by
              simp [volume_univ]
